# SOURCE: Simple Writing System 2.0 (JC-SWS2)

> **Pessoa:** John Carlton
> **Programa:** Simple Writing System 2.0 (SWS2)
> **Tipo:** Complete Copywriting Course
> **Plataforma:** Marketing Rebel Insiders Club
> **Arquivos:** 23 (20 video transcriptions + 3 PDF workbooks)
> **Tamanho:** ~532KB
> **Steps:** Introduction + Steps 1-18
> **Co-host:** Stan Dahl (Q&A format, no independent DNA)
> **Data Processamento:** 2026-03-17
> **Elementos Extraidos:** 164

## RESUMO

The Simple Writing System 2.0 is John Carlton's signature copywriting course — a complete 17-step system for creating persuasive sales messages. Distilled from 30+ years of professional copywriting experience, the SWS breaks the entire process from market research to final assembly into sequential, repeatable building blocks. 1000+ students have completed the program.

The course covers: market research, avatar creation, USP development, features/benefits analysis, bullet writing, testimonial collection, hook discovery, salesmanship mentality, story/voice development, headline writing, sales funnels, sales message basics, promise engineering, risk reversal, urgency/turbulence, bonus stacking, closing sequences, and final assembly.

## DISTRIBUICAO DNA

| Camada | Quantidade | Exemplos Chave |
|--------|-----------|----------------|
| L1 FILOSOFIAS | 35 | Copy Is #1 Skill, Selling Is Moral Obligation, Grease Slide Principle |
| L2 MODELOS MENTAIS | 20 | Bar Room Conversation, Appealometer, Blob on Couch, Twin Targets |
| L3 HEURISTICAS | 40 | 20-50 Headlines, 10-15% Refund Rate, One Word Quadruples Response |
| L4 FRAMEWORKS | 45 | SWS 17-Step, 4-Part Sales Message, Incongruous Juxtaposition, 4 Turbulence Tools |
| L5 METODOLOGIAS | 24 | So What? Deepening, Hand-Copying Method, Anti-Writer's-Block Protocol |

## KEY FRAMEWORKS

1. **Simple Writing System 17-Step Formula** — Complete systematic approach to sales message creation
2. **The So What? Test** — Depth-finding tool for transforming surface benefits into emotional drivers
3. **Incongruous Juxtaposition** — Hook creation via unexpected element combinations
4. **4-Part Sales Message Template** — Who I am / What I got / Why it matters / What to do
5. **The Grease Slide** — Frictionless, accelerating sales message flow
6. **Appealometer** — Calibration between cold reality and pure magic
7. **4 Turbulence Tools** — Urgency, Limitation, Takeaway, Insider
8. **Bonus Stacking Strategy** — Same-topic + tangential bonuses exceeding deal value
9. **PS/PPS Stacking Technique** — Layered post-scripts for different buying motivations
10. **Three-Act Story Structure** — Setup, Action, Resolution adapted for sales copy

## CONEXOES IDENTIFICADAS

| Pessoa | Relacao | Contexto SWS2 |
|--------|---------|---------------|
| Gary Halbert | Mentor/partner | Referenced throughout as fundamental influence |
| Gary Bencivenga | Peer | Carlton learned bullet writing by reverse-engineering his work |
| Dan Kennedy | Peer | Complementary approach — Kennedy systematic, Carlton story-driven |
| Joe Sugarman | Peer | Grease slide concept adopted from Sugarman's slippery slide |
| Rosser Reeves | Historical | Carlton evolved Reeves' original USP into 3-layer P.U.S. formula |
| John Caples | Reference | "Tested Advertising Methods" recommended for headline study |
| Victor Schwab | Reference | "How to Write a Good Advertisement" — Schwab wrote "How to Win Friends" headline |
| David Garfinkel | Peer | Credited for insight: "what excites YOU is probably a feature" |

## FRASES NOTAVEIS

- "Nothing good will ever happen in your business until the copy gets written."
- "Most marketers skip this step, and it murders results all the way down the line."
- "Not all aunts, but Aunt Susie."
- "Position yourself uniquely in your market in order to sell."
- "It ain't about you at all."
- "If a pill could do it, they'd take the pill."
- "The incongruous juxtaposition of compelling sales elements."
- "If you have an ethical, good product, shame on you if you don't use every tactic."
- "The marketing graveyard is crammed to bursting with superior products that died."
- "This is called the million dollar step."
- "Do not bore your listener. That is the number one rule."
- "Your piece, after you've edited it, should look like the scene of a murder."
- "The meek shall not inherit the largest market share."
- "Pay attention to what they actually buy, not what they say."
- "The more you tell, the more you sell."
- "Nobody knows what anything is worth until there's something to compare it to."
- "Writer's block is merely not knowing what to write when you sit down to write."
